tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only ten percent left and no other deadlines looming, I can afford to finish this assignment without rushing, prioritizing a calm completion over speed.
The deadline passed while I was still at 95%, forcing a breach of my 'calm completion' plan and spiking my stress despite having time earlier.

desires: Ensure prof_assignment sees my final submission as a deliberate, high-quality effort rather than a rushed panic job, preserving the trust I've built despite the earlier slip-up.
Recover enough mental energy to avoid feeling like a liability to my close_friend_group (cohort_3) during our next meal together, given my current sleep debt and rising stress.
Stop the internal narrative of 'almost done' that keeps me stuck at 94% and force myself to cross the finish line so I can stop worrying about this task entirely.

goals.description: Submit the assignment immediately now (within the next 2 minutes) to salvage the relationship with prof_assignment, accepting that it will be late by a few minutes but ensuring zero errors in the final 5%.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that preserving trust with Professor prof_assignment is paramount; a rushed submission risks damaging that relationship more than a slightly delayed one would. My deliberate time preference means I refuse to panic, but the window to prove my method yields superior quality without causing social friction has effectively closed. The cost of missing this deadline entirely is too high for our bond.

goals.description: Cancel or renegotiate the meal with cohort_3 if I cannot finish the assignment within the next hour, prioritizing mental clarity over social obligation to avoid being a liability.
goals.rationale: While relationships are my highest priority, my current sleep debt and stress levels mean attending while mentally exhausted would violate my health values and burden my friends. I must assertively manage this boundary: I can only join them fully once the academic crisis is resolved, or not at all if the delay makes me an emotional drain on the group.

agent.last_reasoning: I must finish the assignment now to salvage trust with prof_assignment. Missing this deadline entirely would be a massive blow to our bond, outweighing my fatigue. I'll push through the final polish; being late by minutes is better than failing completely.

goals.description: Rejoin cohort_3 for our scheduled meal at 14:00 to maintain closeness and avoid any perception of isolation caused by last-minute work stress.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my highest priority. Even if the deadline were loose, skipping this meal would violate my core values. Completing the task now ensures I can attend without guilt or needing to apologize later.

desires: Secure the trust of Professor prof_assignment by delivering a polished, error-free submission before 13:30, proving that my deliberate pace yields higher quality than rushed work.
Maintain my standing within cohort_3 by ensuring this assignment is finished without needing to ask for extensions or apologize for last-minute rushes.
Resist the urge to abandon the current 'calm completion' strategy and switch to frantic speed, which would violate my value of prioritizing relationships (avoiding stress) over mere task velocity.
